ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF PAIGE BARHAM'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PRIVILEGE LOG, INTERROGATORY RESPONSES, REQUEST FOR ADMISSION RESPONSE, AND FOR SANCTIONS HEARING SET FOR MAY-15-2018 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-31-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, PLAINTIFF PAIGE BARHAM'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PRIVILEGE LOG, INTERROGATORY RESPONSES, REQUEST FOR ADMISSION RESPONSE, AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 7, 2018 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO ENGAGE IN MEANINGFUL FACE TO FACE MEET AND CONFER IN AN EFFORT TO RESOLVE ALL OF THEIR DISAGREEMENTS ABOUT THE DISCOVERY THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION. NO LATER THAN JUNE 4, 2018 COUNSEL MUST MEET IN PERSON WHERE THEY MUST DISCUSS EACH DISCOVERY REQUEST THAT IS IN DISPUTE. IF ANY DISCOVERY DISPUTES REMAIN AFTER THE MEETING, BY 4PM ON JUNE 6, 2018 COUNSEL FOR MS. BARHAM MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IDENTIFYING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, EACH DISCOVERY REQUEST THAT REMAINS IN DISPUTE. FOR GUIDANCE OF THE PARTIES IN THEIR MEETING, THE COURT: 1) EXPECTS THAT THE PARTIES WILL MAKE GOOD FAITH EFFORTS TO AGREE ON THE LANGUAGE OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER; 2) BELIEVES THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE WITHHELD ON THE GROUNDS OF PRIVILEGE THAT ARE DATED PRIOR TO THE SERVICE OF THE COMPLAINT ON DEFENDANTS NEED TO BE IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG; 3) ADMONISHES DEFENDANTS THAT A GREAT MAJORITY OF THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT BY MS. BARHAM IS PERMISSIBLE AND THAT FURTHER RESPONSES BY DEFENDANTS SHOULD INCLUDE ALL INFORMATION WITHIN THE POSSESSION, CUSTODY AND CONTROL OF DEFENDANTS AS OF THE DATE OF THE RESPONSES; AND 4) ADVISES MS. BARHAM THAT THE PERSONNEL FILE AND OTHER PERSONAL INFORMATION OF THE DRIVER NEED NOT BE PROVIDED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PAIGE BARHAM'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, PRIVILEGE LOG, INTERROGATORY RESPONSES, REQUEST FOR ADMISSION RESPONSE, AND FOR SANCTIONS IS TAKEN OFF-CALENDAR PER THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. Plaintiff Paige Barham'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents, Privilege Log, Interrogatory Responses, Request For Admission Response, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Continued To Jun-07-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Paige Barham's motion to compel production of documents, privilege log, interrogatory responses, request for admission response, and for sanctions is continued to June 7, 2018 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in meaningful face to face meet and confer in an effort to resolve all of their disagreements about the discovery that is the subject of this motion. No later than June 4, 2018 counsel must meet in person where they must discuss each discovery request that is in dispute. If any discovery disputes remain after the meeting, by 4pm on June 6, 2018 counsel for Ms. Barham must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for defendants identifying, without argument, each discovery request that remains in dispute. For guidance of the parties in their meeting, the court: 1) expects that the parties will make good faith efforts to agree on the language of a protective order; 2) believes that all responsive documents that are withheld on the grounds of privilege that are dated prior to the service of the complaint on defendants need to be identified in a privilege log; 3) admonishes defendants that a great majority of the discovery sought by Ms. Barham is permissible and that further responses by defendants should include all information within the possession, custody and control of defendants as of the date of the responses; and 4) advises Ms. Barham that the personnel file and other personal information of the driver need not be provided. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
